principles: Suffering Produces a Chain Reaction That Ends in Hope
The chain is sequential: skip endurance and you don’t get character; skip character and your hope has no foundation.
Christ Died for the Ungodly — Not the Improved
The Gift Is Not Like the Trespass — It’s Greater
One Act of Obedience Reverses One Act of Rebellion

Leaders, founders, and parents function as representative heads — their defining choices cascade through everyone — connected to them. The sober reality is that one decision can set a trajectory for many. The hopeful reality is that one righteous act can reset it.

God is revealed as the initiator of reconciliation. He didn’t wait for humanity to seek peace — He sent Christ while we — were still enemies. His love is demonstrated, not just declared  -  the cross is the proof, not the promise of proof. And
His grace is not measured or proportional; it superabounds. He is a God whose remedy is always greater than the — disease, whose generosity outpaces the damage.
godShot: God is revealed as the initiator of reconciliation. He didn't wait for humanity to seek peace  -  He sent Christ while we were still enemies. His love is demonstrated, not just declared  -  the cross is the proof, not the promise of proof. And His grace is not measured or proportional; it superabounds. He is a God whose remedy is always greater than the disease, whose generosity outpaces the damag

title: Dead to Sin, Alive to God
content: If grace increases where sin increases, should we keep sinning? Paul's answer is visceral: absolutely not. Baptism into Christ means co-death and co-resurrection  -  the old self was crucified, and the believer is now alive to God.
principles: Romans • Page 6
You Cannot Benefit from a Death You Refuse to Die
Know, Count, Present — The Sequence of Freedom
You Are Always a Slave — The Question Is to Whom
The Wages of Sin vs. the Gift of God — Earned vs. Given

God is revealed as the one who does not leave the justified person in the same condition. He doesn’t merely declare — righteous and walk away  -  He breaks the dominion of sin. His grace is not permission to remain enslaved; it's the power to live free. He buries the old self and raises a new one, and His gift  -  eternal life in Christ  -  is categorically differen
godShot: God is revealed as the one who does not leave the justified person in the same condition. He doesn't merely declare righteous and walk away  -  He breaks the dominion of sin. His grace is not permission to remain enslaved; it's the power to live free. He buries the old self and raises a new one, and His gift  -  eternal life in Christ  -  is categorically different from anything that can be earned

title: The War Within
content: Paul explores the believer's relationship to the law and the agonizing internal conflict: the law is holy, but sin exploits it. The person who wants to do good finds evil right there with them.
principles: Romans • Page 7
The Law Is Good, but It Cannot Make You Good
Sin Uses Good Things as Weapons
The Divided Self Is a Real Experience — Not a Sign of Faithlessness
The Cry for Rescue Is the Right Ending

God is revealed as the author of a holy, righteous, and good law — and as the one who knows the law alone cannot — save. He isn't surprised by the Chapter 7 struggle; He designed it to drive people toward Christ. The law's inability to produce righteousness isn't a design flaw  -  it's a design feature, meant to make the cry for rescue both inevitable and honest.
godShot: God is revealed as the author of a holy, righteous, and good law  -  and as the one who knows the law alone cannot save. He isn't surprised by the Chapter 7 struggle; He designed it to drive people toward Christ. The law's inability to produce righteousness isn't a design flaw  -  it's a design feature, meant to make the cry for rescue both inevitable and honest. God's holiness set the standard. G

title: No Condemnation, No Separation
content: The summit of Romans: no condemnation for those in Christ, life in the Spirit, adoption as children, the Spirit's intercession, God working all things together for good, and the unbreakable declaration  -  nothing can separate us from God's love. Romans • Page 8
principles: No Condemnation Doesn’t Mean No Struggle — It Means No Verdict
The Mind Set on the Spirit Is Life and Peace
Present Suffering Is Real but Not Comparable to Coming Glory
God Works All Things Together — Not All Things Are Good
The Golden Chain of Salvation Cannot Be Broken
God begins, He finishes.
Nothing Can Separate — The List Is Exhaustive
Romans • Page 9 — ❤ GOD SHOT

God is revealed in His fullest relational commitment. He condemns sin in the flesh through His Son so He can declare — no condemnation over His people. He sends His Spirit to indwell, testify to adoption, and intercede with groans too deep for words. He foreknows, predestines, calls, justifies, and glorifies  -  every verb is His. He is for us, which settles every ac
godShot: God is revealed in His fullest relational commitment. He condemns sin in the flesh through His Son so He can declare no condemnation over His people. He sends His Spirit to indwell, testify to adoption, and intercede with groans too deep for words. He foreknows, predestines, calls, justifies, and glorifies  -  every verb is His. He is for us, which settles every accusation. He did not spare His ow

title: God’s Sovereign Freedom in Election
content: Paul, anguished over unbelieving Israel, confronts the hardest question: has God's word failed? No  -  because God's promises were never based on ethnic descent but on sovereign election.
principles: The Promise Line and the Biological Line Are Not the Same
God’s Mercy Is Not Owed — It’s Sovereign
God’s compassion.
The Clay Doesn’t Get to Question the Potter
God becomes a refusal to accept His authority. Intellectual humility means acknowledging that some answers belong to
God alone.
God Endures Vessels of Wrath to Display Mercy on Vessels of Mercy
Arminian traditions.) — (Interpretive leap flagged)
Romans • Page 10 — ❤ GOD SHOT

God is revealed as absolutely sovereign in His election — free to choose, free to harden, free to show mercy, and — accountable to no one for these decisions. Yet His sovereignty is not arbitrary cruelty; it serves to reveal the full spectrum of His character  -  wrath, patience, power, and the riches of His glory. He is a potter who has the right to make from the
godShot: God is revealed as absolutely sovereign in His election  -  free to choose, free to harden, free to show mercy, and accountable to no one for these decisions. Yet His sovereignty is not arbitrary cruelty; it serves to reveal the full spectrum of His character  -  wrath, patience, power, and the riches of His glory. He is a potter who has the right to make from the same lump of clay vessels for dif

title: The Accessible Word and the Beautiful Feet
content: Paul's heart breaks for Israel  -  they have zeal but not according to knowledge. They pursued righteousness by works and stumbled over Christ. But the word of faith is near, accessible to all who call on the Lord.
principles: Zeal Without Knowledge Is Dangerous, Not Admirable
Christ Is the End of the Law for Righteousness
The Word Is Near — Salvation Is Accessible
Beautiful Feet Require Being Sent
Romans • Page 11 — ❤ GOD SHOT

God is revealed as radically accessible. He has not hidden the way of salvation behind intellectual puzzles, — geographic pilgrimages, or heroic achievements. He placed the word of faith on the lips and in the heart  -  the nearest possible location. He extends His hands all day long to a disobedient and contrary people, which means His patience outlasts thei
godShot: God is revealed as radically accessible. He has not hidden the way of salvation behind intellectual puzzles, geographic pilgrimages, or heroic achievements. He placed the word of faith on the lips and in the heart  -  the nearest possible location. He extends His hands all day long to a disobedient and contrary people, which means His patience outlasts their stubbornness. He is a God who keeps rea

title: The Olive Tree and the Mystery of Israel
content: Has God rejected Israel? Emphatically no. A remnant remains by grace. Gentile inclusion is not replacement but grafting in. Israel's hardening is partial and temporary  -  and when the fullness of the Gentiles comes in, all Israel will be saved.
principles: God Always Preserves a Remnant — Even When It’s Invisible
If the Root Is Holy, the Branches Will Be
Arrogance Toward the Broken-Off Branches Is Self-Destructive
God’s Gifts and Calling Are Irrevocable
Romans • Page 12 — ❤ GOD SHOT
God is revealed as the master strategist of redemptive history. He used Israel’s stumbling to bring salvation to the

Gentiles, and He will use Gentile inclusion to provoke Israel to jealousy — and ultimately to salvation. His severity — and kindness are both real, both purposeful. And when Paul tries to articulate the fullness of God's plan, he collapses into worship: "Oh, the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God!" His judgments are unsearchable, His paths beyond trac

He used Israel's stumbling to bring salvation to the Gentiles, and He will use Gentile inclusion to provoke Israel to jealousy  -  and ultimately to salvation. His severity and kindness are both real, both purposeful. And when Paul tries to articulate the fullness of God's plan, he collapses into worship: "Oh, the depth of the riches

title: The Living Sacrifice and the Transformed
content: Community The "therefore" chapter: because of all God has done (chapters 1-11), here is how to live. Present your body as a living sacrifice. Be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Use your gifts. Love without hypocrisy.
principles: Worship Is a Body on the Altar, Not Just a Song in the Air
Transformation Happens Through Mind Renewal, Not Behavior Modification
Sober Self-Assessment Is a Spiritual Discipline
Love Must Be Genuine — Hypocrisy Is the Counterfeit
Romans • Page 13
Overcome Evil with Good — Never with Retaliation
God is revealed as the one whose mercies are so vast that the only reasonable response is a whole life offered back.

He is not a God who saves and then leaves people to figure out ethics on their own — He provides both the — theological foundation and the ethical blueprint. He assigns gifts purposefully to build a functioning body. He reserves vengeance for Himself not because He's indifferent to injustice, but because He's the only one qualified to execute it perfectly.

He assigns gifts purposefully to build a functioning body. He reserves vengeance for Himself not because He's indifferent to

title: Submission, Love, and Wakefulness
content: Paul addresses the believer's relationship to governing authorities, the centrality of love as the fulfillment of the law, and the urgency of living as though the night is almost over and the day is at hand.
principles: Governing Authority Exists Within God’s Ordering — Not Outside It
Love Fulfills the Entire Law
Romans • Page 14
Live with Eschatological Urgency — The Night Is Almost Over
The day is at hand. Live as though the light is about to break. — ❤ GOD SHOT

God is revealed as the sovereign organizer of human society — governing structures exist because He established — them, and He uses them as instruments of order, even when imperfect. But He is also the God of the approaching dawn. He is bringing a day, and the nearness of that day is the engine of ethical urgency. His love is both the content of the law and the
godShot: God is revealed as the sovereign organizer of human society  -  governing structures exist because He established them, and He uses them as instruments of order, even when imperfect. But He is also the God of the approaching dawn. He is bringing a day, and the nearness of that day is the engine of ethical urgency. His love is both the content of the law and the power to fulfill it.

title: The Freedom and Limits of Conscience
content: Paul addresses disputable matters  -  eating, observing days  -  where believers with different convictions share the same community. The strong must not despise the weak; the weak must not judge the strong.
principles: Disputable Matters Require Charity, Not Uniformity
Each Person Must Be Fully Convinced in Their Own Mind
Don’t Use Your Freedom to Destroy Someone Else
Romans • Page 15
The Kingdom of God Is Not About Externals

God is revealed as the Lord of each individual conscience. He is the master before whom each servant stands or falls — -  and He is able to make them stand. He doesn't need human enforcers policing disputable matters. His kingdom is not fragile enough to be threatened by dietary choices; it is defined by the substance of the Spirit's work. He is the
God who judges, which means no one else needs to fill that role.

He doesn't need human enforcers policing disputable matters. His kingdom is not fragile enough to be threatened by dietary choices; it is defined by the substance of the Spirit's work. He is the God who judges, which means no one else needs to

title: The Strong Bear with the Weak, and the Nations
content: Worship Together Paul calls the strong to bear the weaknesses of the weak without self-pleasing. Christ modeled this. The chapter unfolds into a vision of Jew and Gentile worshiping together, fulfilling Scripture's promise that the nations would praise God.
principles: Strength Exists to Serve Weakness, Not to Celebrate Itself
Endurance and Encouragement Come Through the Scriptures
Romans • Page 16
The Gospel Goes to Unreached Territory — Not Where Someone Else Has Built
God is revealed as the God of endurance, encouragement, and hope — He is the source of all three. He is also the

God of the nations: Paul strings together Old Testament quotations to show that Gentile inclusion was always God’s — plan, not a backup strategy. The nations praising God alongside Israel is the fulfillment of promises made centuries before Paul was born. God's patience in unfolding His plan across millennia reveals a sovereignty that doesn't rush and a faithfulnes
godShot: God is revealed as the God of endurance, encouragement, and hope  -  He is the source of all three. He is also the God of the nations: Paul strings together Old Testament quotations to show that Gentile inclusion was always God's plan, not a backup strategy. The nations praising God alongside Israel is the fulfillment of promises made centuries before Paul was born. God's patience in unfolding His

title: The Community That Carried the Gospel
content: Paul's closing: a long list of personal greetings revealing the real, diverse network of people who made the gospel's advance possible. He warns against divisive people, gives final encouragement, and closes with a doxology.
principles: Name the People — The Gospel Advances Through Specific Individuals
Watch Out for Those Who Cause Divisions
The God of Peace Will Crush Satan — And Soon
Romans • Page 17 — ❤ GOD SHOT
God is revealed as the One who has been working through every named person, every household, every co-laborer.

He is the God of peace who crushes enemies. He is the one who establishes believers by the gospel. The final — doxology declares Him as the "only wise God" to whom glory belongs forever  -  the same God who conceived the mystery of the gospel before time, revealed it through the prophets, and now makes it known to all nations for the obedience of faith.
Romans • Page 18

He is the one who establishes believers by the gospel. The final doxology declares Him as the "only wise God" to whom glory belongs forever  -  the same God who conceived the mystery of the gospel before time, revealed it through the prophets, a

title: Corinthians
content: Paul writes to a fractured church obsessed with status and eloquence. Where does true wisdom and unity come from when a community splinters around human leaders?  - 1 -
principles: Unity Is Found in the Cross, Not in Leaders
God Chooses the Foolish to Shame the Wise
The Message Must Not Be Dressed Up to Impress
Your Calling Is the Evidence, Not Your Resume
godShot: God is intentionally subversive  -  He doesn't just tolerate weakness, He selects it as His preferred instrument. This is not God making do with what's available; it's God actively choosing the foolish, the weak, the low, and the despised to nullify what the world considers powerful (vv. 27-28). He is a God who structurally eliminates human boasting by designing a salvation that no one can take cr

title: The Spirit Reveals What Eyes Cannot See
content: Paul contrasts human wisdom with divine revelation. How does anyone actually come to understand the deep things of God, and why do so many miss it?
principles: Trembling Doesn’t Disqualify the Messenger
Paul modeled. His weakness ensured the Corinthians’ faith rested on God’s power, not Paul’s charisma. Nervous — delivery grounded in truth outperforms polished delivery grounded in self.  - 2 -
The Spirit Is the Only Access Point to God’s Mind
Spirit is like a high-resolution screen with no signal. Pair study with dependence.
The Natural Person Cannot Receive Spiritual Truth
godShot: God is deliberately hidden from the self-sufficient and revealed to the Spirit-dependent. He prepared things no eye has seen and no ear has heard (v. 9), then gave His own Spirit as the sole decoder. God is not obscure because He is distant  -  He is obscure to the proud because His revelation system bypasses human self-reliance entirely. 1 Corinthians 3  -  You Are God's Building, Not Your Own Th

title: You Are God’s Building, Not Your Own
content: The Corinthians are still infants  -  divided over leaders and inflated by worldly thinking. Who actually builds the church, and what happens when builders use the wrong materials?
principles: Spiritual Immaturity Shows Up in Division
Planters and Waterers Are Nothing — God Gives Growth
Build with Materials That Survive Fire
You Are God’s Temple — Do Not Destroy It

See What Earnestness Godly Grief Has Produced in You
Repentance has a body.
Godly Confrontation, When Received Well, Deepens Relationship

God orchestrates grief for redemptive purposes. He grieved the Corinthians through Paul’s letter — not to punish but — to produce repentance leading to salvation without regret (v. 10). God is not a sadist who enjoys pain; He is a surgeon who cuts precisely where healing requires it.
Paul holds up the Macedonian churches as an example of radical generosity. What does generosity look like when it — flows from grace rather than obligation?

First They Gave Themselves to the Lord
Christ Became Poor So That Through His Poverty You Might Become Rich

God. You are a channel, not a reservoir. The more you let flow through, the more flows in.

He could heal instantly but sometimes chooses sustained presence over instant relief, because His glory is more visible in endurance than in escape. He is perfecting His power through it. 2 Corinthians 13  -  Examine Yourselves Paul's closing chap

title: Examine Yourselves

The God of Love and Peace Will Be with You
